title: Keiko's Big Feelings Day
synopsis: When Keiko's favorite teddy bear goes missing, she experiences big feelings of sadness and worry. With help from her diverse family, she learns that it's okay to feel scared and that asking for help can solve problems.

pages.text: Keiko loved her brown teddy bear named Mr. Fuzzy. Every morning, she would hug him tight and feel happy. But today, when Keiko woke up, Mr. Fuzzy was gone! She looked in her bed, but he wasn't there.

illustration.description: A young Japanese girl with short black hair in pajamas looking confused and concerned while searching through rumpled bedsheets. Her bedroom has bright yellow walls with colorful toys scattered around. The bed has rainbow-striped sheets and fluffy pillows.
illustration.alt: Keiko searching her bed for her missing teddy bear

pages.text: Keiko felt sad and worried. Her eyes filled with tears. "Where is Mr. Fuzzy?" she asked. She looked under her pillow and behind her books, but still no teddy bear. Keiko's lip began to tremble.

illustration.description: Keiko with tears in her dark eyes, kneeling on the floor looking under furniture. Her expression shows genuine sadness and worry. The room is bright with red and blue accents, toys on shelves, and morning sunlight streaming through a window.
illustration.alt: Sad Keiko crying while searching for her teddy bear

pages.text: Mama heard Keiko crying and came into the room. Mama had warm brown skin and kind eyes. "What's wrong, sweetie?" Mama asked, sitting on the bed. "I can't find Mr. Fuzzy anywhere!" Keiko said, feeling scared that he was lost forever.

illustration.description: A caring Black woman with natural curly hair in a cozy robe sitting on the colorful bed, reaching out to comfort crying Keiko. The scene shows warmth and love with soft morning light, bright primary colors, and a nurturing atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting sad Keiko on the bed

pages.text: "Let's look for Mr. Fuzzy together," Mama said gently. "Sometimes when we feel scared, it helps to have someone help us." Keiko wiped her tears and felt a little bit brave. Papa came to help too. Papa had kind eyes behind his glasses and gave Keiko a warm hug.

illustration.description: A loving family scene with Keiko between her Black mother and Asian father, all looking determined to search together. Papa wears glasses and has a gentle smile. The room is filled with bright colors - red toy boxes, yellow walls, blue carpet - showing unity and teamwork.
illustration.alt: Keiko with both parents ready to search for the teddy bear

pages.text: They searched the living room where Grandma Rosa was reading. Grandma had silver hair and a big smile. "Have you checked the laundry basket?" she asked. They looked there, and guess what? There was Mr. Fuzzy, all clean and fluffy!

illustration.description: A joyful discovery scene in a bright living room where an elderly Latina grandmother with silver hair points toward a wicker laundry basket. Mr. Fuzzy, a brown teddy bear, is visible among colorful clean clothes. The room has warm orange and green tones with family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: Grandma Rosa pointing to Mr. Fuzzy in the laundry basket

pages.text: Keiko felt so happy and relieved! She hugged Mr. Fuzzy tight and then hugged Mama, Papa, and Grandma Rosa. "Thank you for helping me!" she said with a big smile. "It's okay to feel sad and scared sometimes," Mama said. "And it's always okay to ask for help when you need it."

illustration.description: A heartwarming group hug scene with Keiko in the center holding her brown teddy bear, surrounded by her diverse family all smiling warmly. Everyone's faces show pure joy and love. The background features bright, cheerful colors with a cozy home setting filled with family warmth.
illustration.alt: Happy family group hug with Keiko holding Mr. Fuzzy

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Keiko feel when she couldn't find Mr. Fuzzy?
questions.explanation: Keiko felt sad and scared when she couldn't find her beloved teddy bear Mr. Fuzzy.

questions.question: Who helped Keiko look for Mr. Fuzzy?
questions.explanation: Keiko's whole family helped her - Mama, Papa, and Grandma Rosa all worked together to find Mr. Fuzzy.

questions.question: Where did they find Mr. Fuzzy?
questions.explanation: Grandma Rosa suggested looking in the laundry basket, where they found Mr. Fuzzy clean and fluffy.

questions.question: What did Mama teach Keiko about feelings?
choices: It's okay to feel sad and ask for help
questions.explanation: Mama taught Keiko that it's okay to feel sad and scared sometimes, and that it's always okay to ask for help.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Keiko felt ____ when she couldn't find Mr. Fuzzy. Her ____ helped her look everywhere. They found the teddy bear in the ____ basket.

pages.title: Feelings Words
pages.text: When we lose something special, we might feel ____. When we find it again, we feel ____. It's ____ to ask for help when we need it.
